translation.en: We will fly the kite high.

content.sentence: Chúng ta sẽ ___ chiếc diều lên cao.

notes_md:
**thả** = to release or let something go so it’s free. You use it when you stop holding, keeping, or controlling something.

It often goes with animals, objects, or things that can move away: fish, birds, balloons, hair, even prisoners. It can also mean to let something fall lightly or naturally (not throw hard).  


Common combos: *thả lỏng* (relax your body), *thả tóc* (let your hair down), *thả thính* (slang: casually flirt or “drop hints” online).  

Close to **buông** (let go), but *buông* can feel more emotional; opposite is **giữ** (hold/keep).
notes:

It often goes with animals, objects, or things that can move away: fish, birds, balloons, hair, even prisoners. It can also mean to let something fall lightly or naturally (not throw hard).  

Common combos: thả lỏng (relax your body), thả tóc (let your hair down), thả thính (slang: casually flirt or “drop hints” online).

example.vi: Cô ấy thả con chim ra ngoài.

definitions.vi: Để cho cái gì đó đi, thả ra hoặc bỏ xuống.
definitions.es: Dejar ir, soltar o dejar caer algo.
definitions.en: To let go, release, or drop something.

exampleSentencesTranslated: I will release this fish into the river so it can live freely.
Remember to release the balloons into the sky at the end of the party.
Every morning, I usually release the birds from the cage so they can fly around.
exampleSentences: Anh sẽ thả con cá này về sông để nó được sống tự do.
Chị nhớ thả bóng bay lên trời vào cuối buổi tiệc nhé.
Mỗi sáng, em thường thả chim ra khỏi lồng cho chúng bay chơi.

translation.en: They will drop the fruit on the ground.

content.sentence: Họ sẽ ___ trái cây xuống đất.
